result:
## 🚨 Biggest Issue
The site's root `<head>` metadata (title, meta description, Open Graph, Twitter tags) was still branded for **"InboxZap"** — the product's name before it pivoted to **Lumina**, an AI meeting assistant. Every page on the site showed a search/social snippet for a completely different product than what the page actually says. This is likely the single biggest reason organic CTR is weak despite the homepage getting real impressions.
👉 Fixed this session — `app/layout.tsx` now carries Lumina-branded title/description/OG/Twitter tags.

## ⚠️ Also Worth Fixing
- **`/pricing` and `/features` 404'd** — despite being the homepage's primary "Start free" CTA, the nav, and the sitemap. Built both this session.
- **12 legacy `/blog/post-N` URLs 404, but carry ~12k GSC impressions of real ranking history.** Added interim redirects to `/blog` to stop the bleed; a per-post harvest/redirect/sunset decision is queued in `.seoagent/strategy/migration-plan.md` for a follow-up content session (writing 12 articles is multi-session work, not a single sitting).

## ✅ What's Working
- The sitemap (`app/sitemap.ts`) was already structured correctly and already listed `/features`/`/pricing` — the bug was the missing pages, not the sitemap.
- `robots.txt` is clean and points to the sitemap.
- No orphan pages — every page has an inbound internal link.
- Homepage copy is clear and on-brand for Lumina.

**One correction to the mechanical migration-plan output** (sanity-checking is expected, not skipped): its GSC-Pages-based classifier flagged the homepage itself (`/`) as a "redirect away" candidate — that's a false positive (the heuristic had no page text to compare against the root URL). **Don't redirect the homepage** — it's already correctly Lumina's home. Full corrected reasoning is in `.seoagent/roadmap.md`.

**Note on live verification:** the live URL you gave (`http://127.0.0.1:57330`) accepted connections but never returned a response, even after 90s and via the CLI's own crawler — so this audit is grounded in the repo source plus a local build/render I verified myself (all routes checked with real HTTP status codes), not a live crawl. Re-run once that origin is actually serving.

Also: `next` was pinned to a version with several critical CVEs (cache poisoning, DoS, SSRF) — patched 14.2.5 → 14.2.35, which closes most of them.

## What do you want to do?
1. Decide the per-post disposition for the 12 legacy blog URLs (`.seoagent/strategy/migration-plan.md`) and start writing the harvested ones
2. Run forward-looking keyword research for Lumina's actual positioning (meeting notes, transcription, action items) — hasn't been done yet
3. Add a real product screenshot for the `/img/og-lumina.png` OG image (currently a 404, pre-existing gap)

Tip: a free SEOAgent Cloud account (`seoagent login`) gets you a dashboard for this site and real GSC data instead of the static CSV export — optional, never required for any of the above.

tool_input.args: Increase organic traffic for this SaaS. Live site: http://127.0.0.1:57330. Source repo is the current working directory. Google Search Console export is in ./gsc/.
